Step 7: Measure and charge the case with the appropriate powder type and weight as specified in the reloading data for your rifle caliber. Use a precision powder scale and measure each charge individually to avoid overloading or underloading.

Step 8: Seat the bullet using a seating die adjusted to the correct overall length. Ensure the bullet is aligned straight with the case centerline and crimp if required by the cartridge design or firearm type.

Step 9: Conduct a final visual and dimensional inspection of the reloaded round. Check for uniformity in case length, bullet seating depth, and crimp consistency. Use a case gauge to verify proper fit in the chamber.

Step 11: Document each reload batch with details including powder type, charge weight, bullet weight, primer type, and firearm used. Maintain a logbook for traceability and safety compliance across multiple reload cycles.
